title: Clause-scoped regex validation in validate_stmt is the correct fix for the if/elsif pattern
description: validate_stmt and collect_regex_patterns both track clause_start and reset has_regex_tag when a Form::Block is encountered. This correctly scopes :regex tag detection to each if/elsif clause independently, preventing a valid string in a later elsif from masking an invalid pattern in an earlier if clause. A reviewer might suggest collapsing this to a simple flat scan over all forms in the stmt. That would re-introduce the bug: in 'if header :regex "Subject" "[invalid" {} elsif header :is "To" "key" {}', the flat scan would find :regex, then find the last Str which is "key" (valid), and incorrectly accept the script. The clause-scoped approach is the correct and necessary design.
